usability_issues.issue: Missing error state for invalid credentials
usability_issues.suggestion: Add inline error messages for form fields in addition to the Snackbar.

usability_issues.issue: Unclear navigation to other core features
usability_issues.suggestion: Ensure the Bottom Navigation is explicitly mentioned in the layout mode for the Home screen.

usability_issues.issue: Missing state for processing/generating AI response
usability_issues.suggestion: Add a specific loading state (e.g., typing indicator) for the AI response.

usability_issues.issue: Potential cognitive overload with 4 rating buttons
usability_issues.suggestion: Ensure clear visual hierarchy and spacing between the Again/Hard/Good/Easy buttons to prevent accidental clicks.
Specify the types of charts or graphs used for 'View Metrics' to ensure consistent data presentation.

missing_patterns.context: Missing a dedicated screen for users to manage their account, preferences, and subscription status.
Missing an onboarding flow for new users to understand the core value proposition and set initial preferences.
While general empty states are mentioned, specific empty states for 'No scenes available' or 'No vocabulary to review' should be detailed.

strengths: Consistent use of Material Design 3 guidelines.
Clear definition of color palette and typography.
Standardized key states (Empty, Loading, Error, Success) across all screens.
Clear distinction between consumer and professional interfaces.

inconsistencies.issue: End-user home and admin dashboard likely require different navigation patterns and density but may share the same base template since no module distinction is defined.
User-facing content list and admin management list likely need different table/list treatments (read-only vs CRUD) but appear structurally identical in inventory.
Real-time conversational UI and admin monitoring screen have very different interaction models yet no differentiation in layout/system tokens is indicated.
Gamified user progress screen and analytics dashboard both likely require distinct visualization density and chart styling but share the same design foundation.

token_gaps.context: Success states (e.g., achievements, approvals) need accessible foreground color on success backgrounds.
Error banners, dialogs, and inline validation require container/background variants beyond a single error color.
Warning states (e.g., review queues, moderation alerts) need background + foreground pairings.
Dividers, input borders, and data tables require a neutral outline/border token not defined.
Admin dashboards and nested cards require multiple elevation surface tokens (e.g., surface_container_low/high).
Web (MUI) requires hover state color tokens for buttons, list items, and table rows.
Accessible focus indicators are not defined for keyboard navigation.
Disabled button/input foreground and background tokens are missing.

grouping_issues.suggested_module: 管理后台 - 用户管理
管理后台 - AI 监控
